title: macaranga coriacea
intro: Macaranga coriacea, also known as the 'Bark Tree', is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Macaranga coriacea include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Macaranga coriacea include tea, infusion, and extract, which can be taken orally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
